claim: Lupus-prone mice infected with curliproducing bacteria have lower autoantibody titers compared to controls.

result.claim: Lupus-prone mice infected with curliproducing bacteria have lower autoantibody titers compared to controls.
result.reasoning: The claim is directly refuted by evidence from paper 1834762, "Amyloid-DNA Composites of Bacterial Biofilms Stimulate Autoimmunity." The abstract of this paper explicitly states, "We also found that the infection of lupus-prone mice with curli-producing bacteria triggered higher autoantibody titers compared to curli-deficient bacteria." This directly contradicts the claim that lupus-prone mice infected with curli-producing bacteria have *lower* autoantibody titers.

evidence.text: Curli-DNA composites triggered production of autoantibodies in lupus-prone mice.
evidence.source: Amyloid-DNA Composites of Bacterial Biofilms Stimulate Autoimmunity.

evidence.text: Bromodomain inhibition leads to a decrease in virulence in a mouse model of infection.
evidence.source: Bromodomain Proteins Contribute to Maintenance of Bloodstream Form Stage Identity in the African Trypanosome

evidence.text: Nlrp12(-/-) mice developed atypical neuroinflammatory symptoms that were characterized by loss of balance.
evidence.source: The NLRP12 Sensor Negatively Regulates Autoinflammatory Disease by Modulating Interleukin-4 Production in T Cells.

evidence.text: Curli-producing bacteria triggered higher autoantibody titers in lupus-prone mice than curli-deficient bacteria.
evidence.source: Amyloid-DNA Composites of Bacterial Biofilms Stimulate Autoimmunity.

evidence.text: Citalopram reduced the appearance of new plaques by 78% in plaque-bearing mice.
evidence.source: An Antidepressant Decreases CSF Aβ Production in Healthy Individuals and in Transgenic AD Mice

evidence.text: In mice on a Western diet, genetically induced deficiency of mast cells reduces levels of inflammatory proteases in serum.
evidence.source: Deficiency and pharmacological stabilization of mast cells reduce diet-induced obesity and diabetes in mice

evidence.text: The muscles of the mice had increased fibrosis.
evidence.source: Inducible depletion of satellite cells in adult, sedentary mice impairs muscle regenerative capacity without affecting sarcopenia

evidence.text: F11r(-/-)Igha(-/-) mice displayed markedly increased susceptibility to acute injury-induced colitis.
evidence.source: Compromised intestinal epithelial barrier induces adaptive immune compensation that protects from colitis.

evidence.text: Citalopram arrested the growth of preexisting amyloid plaques in plaque-bearing mice.
evidence.source: An Antidepressant Decreases CSF Aβ Production in Healthy Individuals and in Transgenic AD Mice

evidence.text: Cefoperazone and streptomycin-pretreated mice become persistently colonized with Clostridium difficile.
evidence.source: Clostridium difficile alters the structure and metabolism of distinct cecal microbiomes during initial infection to promote sustained colonization

evidence.text: In mice on a Western diet, genetically induced deficiency of mast cells reduces levels of inflammatory cytokines in serum.
evidence.source: Deficiency and pharmacological stabilization of mast cells reduce diet-induced obesity and diabetes in mice

evidence.text: Antibiotic treatment induces substantial changes in the gut microbial community of mice susceptible to C. difficile infection.
evidence.source: Antibiotic-induced shifts in the mouse gut microbiome and metabolome increase susceptibility to Clostridium difficile infection

evidence.text: Curli-DNA composites triggered production of autoantibodies in wild-type mice.
evidence.source: Amyloid-DNA Composites of Bacterial Biofilms Stimulate Autoimmunity.

evidence.text: Clindamycin-pretreated mice exhibited undetectable Clostridium difficile infection.
evidence.source: Clostridium difficile alters the structure and metabolism of distinct cecal microbiomes during initial infection to promote sustained colonization

evidence.text: Treatment of mice with different antibiotics resulted in varied susceptibility to C. difficile.
evidence.source: Precision microbiome reconstitution restores bile acid mediated resistance to Clostridium difficile

evidence.text: Expansion of host macrophages prevented the development of alveolar proteinosis in mice transplanted with GM-Csf-receptor-deficient progenitors.
evidence.source: Tissue-resident macrophages self-maintain locally throughout adult life with minimal contribution from circulating monocytes.

evidence.text: NLRP12 deficiency did not cause exacerbated ascending paralysis during experimental autoimmune encephalomyelitis (EAE) in Nlrp12(-/-) mice.
evidence.source: The NLRP12 Sensor Negatively Regulates Autoinflammatory Disease by Modulating Interleukin-4 Production in T Cells.

evidence.text: In mice on a Western diet, genetically induced deficiency of mast cells reduces levels of inflammatory chemokines in serum.
evidence.source: Deficiency and pharmacological stabilization of mast cells reduce diet-induced obesity and diabetes in mice

evidence.text: F11r(-/-)Rag1(-/-) mice exhibited increased microflora-dependent colitis.

evidence.text: Nlrp12(-/-) mice developed atypical neuroinflammatory symptoms that were characterized by ataxia.
evidence.source: The NLRP12 Sensor Negatively Regulates Autoinflammatory Disease by Modulating Interleukin-4 Production in T Cells.
